steps.objective: Determine whether Q4 vendor onboarding created undisclosed related-party or segregation-of-duties exposure.

hypotheses.claim: Vendor VEND-2291 is a related party onboarded without disclosure, creating a conflict-of-interest exposure.
hypotheses.subject: VEND-2291
hypotheses.verdict_recommendation: Self-challenge produced a plausible innocent explanation (no HR-registry linkage) that the supporting evidence alone cannot rule out. Recommend dismissal absent a signed beneficial-owner declaration.

hypotheses.claim: USER-A held create, approve, and pay rights over vendor VEND-3350, breaching the segregation-of-duties policy.
hypotheses.subject: VEND-3350
hypotheses.verdict_recommendation: Three independent records (creation, posting, policy text) corroborate a direct control breach with no counter-evidence found. Recommend admission for CONFIRMED review.

hypotheses.claim: Invoice INV-88213 to VEND-1180 was posted twice across separate batches, overstating expense.
hypotheses.subject: VEND-1180
missing_evidence: Bank statement confirming single vs. double disbursement

hypotheses.claim: Executive travel reimbursements exceeded the policy ceiling without a secondary approver.
hypotheses.subject: Executive travel
hypotheses.verdict_recommendation: Only one weak record found; dossier lacks the approval workflow log needed to test this claim further.

timeline.detail: Proposed from vendor master anomaly scan (related_party_flag blank on high-value vendor).
Proposed from access-rights cross-reference (single user holds 3 conflicting roles).
Proposed from ledger fuzzy-match on (vendor, amount, invoice_no) pairs.
Proposed from expense report threshold scan.
Highest-priority open hypothesis selected for investigation.
Master-change row, onboarding-form gap, and first posting all retrieved.
Self-challenge: attempting to find an innocent explanation before proceeding.
HR registry found no family or ownership linkage between USER-C and VEND-2291 — weakens the related-party claim.
Computed gross exposure ahead of verdict: EUR 42,500.00.
Dismissed: counter-evidence outweighs support absent a signed declaration.
Second-priority hypothesis picked up: SoD override on VEND-3350.
Confirmed single-user control across create, approve, pay — policy explicitly requires 3 distinct users above EUR 10,000.
Computed gross exposure: EUR 61,800.00.
Submitted for admission: no counter-evidence surfaced across two independent checks.
Third hypothesis picked up: possible duplicate posting on INV-88213.
Two postings of the same invoice number found in separate batches.
Estimated overstatement if both disbursed: EUR 18,400.00.
Ledger cannot distinguish a re-post correction from a genuine double disbursement — routed to auditor for bank-statement judgment.
Fourth hypothesis picked up: executive travel authorization.
Approval workflow log absent from dossier; cannot test further.
Insufficient dossier coverage to test this claim; flagged as a missing-evidence gap rather than dismissed.
Agent stopped: 1 hypothesis awaiting auditor judgment, 1 submitted, 1 dismissed, 1 insufficient.

questions_for_auditor: VEND-1180 / INV-88213: was the second posting a genuine re-disbursement, or a correcting re-post of the same payment?
Should the related-party dismissal on VEND-2291 stand, or is a signed beneficial-owner declaration still being chased outside the dossier?
